summary: Gruber points readers to Tomas Jogin's scathing critique of RealPlayer, endorsing it as devastating and spot-on. The brief post serves as a link recommendation during a quiet period on Daring Fireball. An update notes that Jogin published follow-up posts containing internal memos and emails from RealNetworks employees, adding further weight to the critique.
key_points: Gruber endorses Tomas Jogin's critique of RealPlayer as 'utterly scathing' and 'devastating'
The post is a brief link blog entry pointing readers to external commentary
Follow-up posts from Jogin included internal RealNetworks communications that corroborated the critique
money_quotes: His conclusion is so spot-on, it's devastating.
key_insight: RealPlayer had become so user-hostile that even internal communications from RealNetworks employees couldn't defend its design choices.
